_meta.description: Triodion texts for the 3rd Saturday of Great Lent (Memorial/Soul Saturday)
_meta.source: OCA Department of Liturgical Music and Translations
_meta.note: This is a moveable feast — always the 3rd Saturday of Great Lent. Texts are identical to Soul Saturday 2; only the weekly tone differs (sung in the tone of week 3).

title: Third Saturday of Great Lent — Memorial Saturday

martyrs.label: For the Martyrs (in the Tone of the week)

hymns.text:
The passion-bearers quenched the fire of terrible wickedness
with the streams of their blood,
having kindled the brightness of true piety in all the world;
but the martyrs utterly burned up the falsely-named gods
and their odors and temples,
and shone the most pure light to those on earth.
Enlightened by them, we flee from the darkness of godlessness
and shun the deception of idolatry,
worshipping Christ, Who grants the world great mercy.

Putting on the shield of faith
and strengthening themselves with the sign of the Cross,
Thy saints, O Lord, bravely went of their own accord to torture,
laying low the pride and error of the devil.
By their supplications, O all-powerful God,
send the world peace, and our souls great mercy!

Not rejoicing in earthly things,
but bravely going forth to tortures,
O all-laudable martyrs, you did not fail in your blessed hopes,
but were heirs of the heavenly Kingdom.
Since now you have boldness before God, the Lover of man,
entreat peace for the world, and for our souls, great mercy!

glory.label: For the Departed
glory.text:
I called to mind the Prophet who cried:
"I am earth and ashes";
and again, I looked into the tombs and saw the naked bones.
And I said: "Who then is the king or the soldier,
the rich man or the poor,
the righteous or the sinner?
But give rest, O Lord, to Thy servants with the righteous,
since Thou art the Lover of man!"

prokeimenon1.refrain: Let Thy mercy and Thy truth preserve me forever!

verses.text: I waited patiently for the Lord; He inclined to me and heard my cry.

prokeimenon2.refrain: I said: "Lord, have mercy on me. Heal my soul!"

verses.text: Blessed is he who considers the poor.

idiomelon.label: Idiomelon
idiomelon.text:
Now is the acceptable time,
now is the day of salvation!
In the abundance of Thy mercy visit my soul,
and take away the burden of my sins,
O only Lover of man!

martyrs.label: For the Martyrs
martyrs.text:
Thou art glorified
in the memory of Thy saints, O Christ God.
At their supplications, send down on us Thy great mercy!

verse1.text: To Thee I lift up mine eyes, O Thou Who art enthroned in the heavens! Behold, as the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, as the eyes of a maid to the hand of her mistress, so our eyes look to the Lord our God, till He have mercy upon us.

verse2.text: Have mercy upon us, O Lord, have mercy upon us, for we have had more than enough of contempt. Too long our soul has been sated with the scorn of those who are at ease, the contempt of the proud.

martyrs.label: For the Martyrs
martyrs.text:
Thou art glorified
in the memory of Thy saints, O Christ God.
At their supplications, send down on us Thy great mercy!

theotokion.label: Theotokion
theotokion.text:
O inextinguishable candle,
O righteous throne, O most pure Lady,
pray for the salvation of our souls!

allSaints.label: Troparion
allSaints.text:
O Apostles, Martyrs, and Prophets,
Hierarchs, Holy Monks, and Righteous Ones,
who completed well the fight and kept the faith,
you have boldness before the Savior.
We pray: "Intercede for us with Him, as He is good,
that He may save our souls!"

departed.label: Troparion for the Departed
departed.text:
Remember Thy servants, O Lord, since Thou art good,
and forgive the sins they have committed in life!
For none is sinless but Thee,
Who givest rest to the departed.

theotokion.label: Theotokion
theotokion.text:
O holy Mother of the ineffable Light,
we honor and magnify thee with angelic hymns.
